tldr: Fine-tuned byT5-xl as a causal language model on an expanded ~36k sample dataset with pseudolabeling and external PDF data to achieve 7th place.
summary: The author achieved 7th place by expanding the training data to ~36k samples through translation, external PDF retrieval, and pseudolabeling deduplicated text. They evaluated multiple byT5 variants and found that model size was critical, with byT5-xl performing best. Instead of using a sequence-to-sequence setup, they framed the task as a causal language modeling problem, which outperformed both other T5 variants and fine-tuned LLMs like Mistral and Qwen. The final submission used byT5-xl trained for 3 epochs.
extensive_summary:
## Data Expansion & Preparation
Initial manual sentence alignment yielded minimal gains. The core strategy focused on expanding the training set to four times its original size, culminating in ~36k samples of varying lengths. This was achieved by translating Turkish-to-English for shared non-English AKTs (~6k samples), extracting transliterations and translations from 18 PDF sources including `Innaya_v2.pdf` and historical geography/micronarrative texts (~6k samples), and deduplicating `published_texts.csv` against existing data. The remaining unduplicated text was split into ~30-word strings and pseudolabeled, adding ~18k samples.

## Model Selection & Training
Model scaling proved decisive: `byt5-small << byt5-base << byt5-large ~< byt5-xl`. The task was formulated as a causal language modeling problem rather than sequence-to-sequence, which significantly improved performance. Fine-tuning larger LLMs like Mistral and Qwen underperformed compared to byT5, and other T5 variants also lagged behind. The final submission utilized `byt5-xl` trained for 3 epochs, though `byt5-large` was used for faster iteration during experimentation.
preprocessing: Translation of Turkish-to-English for non-English AKTs
what_worked: Expanding training data to 4x via pseudolabeling and external PDFs
Using causal LM formulation over seq2seq
what_did_not_work: Initial manual sentence alignment had low impact
Fine-tuning Mistral/Qwen and other T5 variants performed worse than byT5
